acknowledge.def: to accept that something is true or exists

actual.def: real and true, not imagined or guessed

admit.def: to agree that something is true, especially when it is bad

advise.def: to tell someone what you think they should do

ambiguous.def: having more than one possible meaning; not clear

apparent.def: easy to see or understand; obvious

appreciate.def: to understand and enjoy something; to be grateful

arrange.def: to put things in a particular order or position

assess.def: to judge or decide the quality or value of something

battery.def: a thing that gives power to phones, toys, and other devices

branch.def: a part of a tree that grows out from the trunk

circumstance.def: a fact or condition that affects a situation

climb.def: to go up something using your hands and feet

compromise.def: an agreement where both sides give up something

connect.def: to join two or more things together

consequence.def: something that happens as a result of an action

contemporary.def: belonging to the present time; modern

controversy.def: a strong disagreement about something that many people have different opinions about

discipline.def: the practice of training people to obey rules

discover.def: to find something for the first time

encourage.def: to give someone hope or confidence

entrance.def: a door or way into a place

equivalent.def: equal in value, amount, or meaning

establish.def: to start or create something that will last

experience.def: knowledge or skill gained from doing something

explicit.def: said or written in a very clear and direct way

extend.def: to make something longer or larger

facility.def: a building or place that is used for a particular activity

fault.def: a mistake or something wrong with something

implication.def: a possible future effect or result of something

impression.def: a feeling or opinion you have about something

incident.def: something that happens, especially something bad

inevitable.def: certain to happen; impossible to avoid

initiative.def: a new plan or project to solve a problem

instruct.def: to teach someone how to do something

interpret.def: to explain the meaning of something

interrupt.def: to stop someone while they are speaking or doing something

limited.def: not very great in amount or size

manipulate.def: to control someone or something in a clever or dishonest way

massive.def: very large in size, amount, or degree

measure.def: to find the size, amount, or level of something

operation.def: a planned activity that has a particular purpose

parliament.def: a group of people who make the laws in some countries

perspective.def: a particular way of thinking about or looking at something

phenomenon.def: something that happens or exists that is unusual or interesting

pleasure.def: a feeling of happiness or enjoyment

positive.def: feeling good and happy about something

prepare.def: to make something ready for use

profound.def: very great or strong; having a deep effect

prospect.def: the possibility that something good might happen in the future

punish.def: to make someone suffer because they did something wrong

rapidly.def: very quickly; at a fast speed

respond.def: to answer someone or react to something

separate.def: to divide things into different parts or groups

sophisticated.def: having a lot of experience and knowledge about the world

specifically.def: for one particular thing or purpose

struggle.def: to try very hard to do something difficult

suggest.def: to tell someone your idea about what they should do

suitable.def: right for a particular purpose or situation

support.def: to help someone by agreeing with them or giving what they need

theory.def: an idea or set of ideas that explains something

treatment.def: the way you behave towards someone or something

ultimately.def: finally, after everything else has been considered

worst.def: more bad than anything else; the opposite of best

sensible.def: having good judgement; practical and reasonable

application.def: a formal request for something, usually in writing

electricity.def: a form of energy that powers lights, computers, and machines

difficulty.def: something that is not easy to do or understand

seriously.def: in a way that shows something is important

assignment.def: a task or piece of work given to someone

organ.def: a part of the body that does a particular job

bunch.def: a group of things of the same kind

associate.def: to connect someone or something with something else in your mind

aspect.def: one part of a situation or subject
